Everything that you say during your presentation, this is why it's got to be scripted, has a purpose. It has a purpose and that purpose is to close the cell at the end. You don't get to sell platform salespeople, first of all. They really, you know, the inexperienced, they do everything you just said. Their whole physiology changes, their facial expressions change, their speed of speaking changes. And they have not let it be seamless and natural. In one-to-one selling, see what should happen that you've engineered is the person should ask you, now how do we do this? How do I get there? They ask for you to close. They ask for the close, which loops all the way back to see permission to sell. So you should really have permission now to complete the sale. You should have permission to start it and you should have permission to complete it. In my own consulting day, I never start the, here's the six things I can do for you and here's how much it's going to cost and here's, I never do that until they ask. And by the way, if they don't ask, I put them on an airplane and send them home and I never even take a whack at it. Yeah, that's exactly right. I did the same thing when I was selling like the $20,000 done for you, things over the phone. I would tell them the whole thing and then they would say, well, how do I go about hiring you? How do I go about doing this? What's the next step? Again, this is a difference between advanced selling that we're teaching here and really quite frankly the 101 stuff that you can learn anywhere. You know, I think we both hit on, you both hit on a point that I think sometimes we take it for granted. We start with permission to sell. That's the first step in our sales process. Well, selling is closing, right? So if we've been granted permission to sell, we've been granted permission to close. So we just need to take them through the process and move them to close because if not, we're just really wasting everybody's time. Like we said, having a nice visit. Yeah, as Zeke said, there's professional salespeople and there's a lot of professional visitors and you don't get paid. There's a professional visitor. It's just that simple. I've got enough friends that I want. Some of the ones I have I want to get rid of. So, you know, let's... Right. So again, I think that one of the key points here is you should structure your presentation so powerfully that they ask you. It's a whole different ballgame than going into, again, a canned close. A canned or also confrontational close. Confrontational close. Yeah, that's even a better word. Because you've got to put the person in the head of the audience and they're not getting out. And I think we need to move past that. And I think that was the impetus for the creation of this program is to move them to, you know, to... It's part of the process. It's part of the process. So, okay, next question. I think sales pro should be capable of hardcore closing under bad circumstances with a lousy prospect. I think you should have that knowledge and you should have that skill. And then you should sort of score yourself at how good you are overall by how much or how little you ever need to use that skill. Right. And again, all that comes down to is scripting, by the way. I mean, if you've got your hard closed script and you're...the guy's not asking for the sale and you can do all of that stuff. But you want to try to obviously avoid doing that. Okay, next question is, when do I close? When do I close the sale? There's a whole bunch of different...some people say, well, as soon as the buyer shows interest, he's giving you a buying signal. Well, I think... Other than what's the next step? What's the next step? So, if they ask you that, you should probably close it. You should probably close. You should probably close. Let's write it up and let's move on. And I think that's important because we're beginning with the unmindful. Once they ask for it, they're like, why don't you stop? I mean, we think that's funny. And awful lot of people, the sales training you go for it is talking past the close. Right? Is literally the person will ask to buy it and somebody will continue to tell them things that you could tell them after money has changed hands. Right. Don't laugh, but we've actually hired those. I've inherited some of those. You'd hear on the call, okay, that sounds great. What's the next steps? And the person just keeps on talking. And you're like, whoa, whoa, whoa, we're getting the credit card and we're moving forward. So it happens, we laugh and we think it's fun. But if you have a sales team, if you have sales people that you're hiring...
